name: Chocolate Banana Lunchbox Cake
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons unsweetened cocoa powder (not Dutch process)
3/4 cup mashed very ripe banana (2 small)
2/3 cup packed light or dark brown sugar
2 tablespoons chopped walnut or pecans (I omitted)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Lightly oil an 8-by-12-inch or 7-by-11-inch baking pan or coat it with nonstick cooking spray.
3. In a mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, cocoa, baking soda, and salt.
4. In another bowl, whisk together the mashed banana, buttermilk, brown sugar, corn syrup, oil, and vanilla extract until well combined.
5. Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients and add the banana mixture; mix with a wooden spoon or rubber spatula just until the dry ingredients are moistened.
6. Transfer the batter to the prepared baking pan and smooth the top.
7. Sprinkle chocolate chips and nuts over the top (I omitted).
8. Bake for about 20 minutes, or until a skewer inserted into the center comes out clean.
9. Let cool in the pan before dusting with confectioner's sugar and cutting into squares.
